subject: Cloud Computing
title: IBM Cloud and the Pivot
description: A quick, practical guide to IBM Cloud and the Pivot.

hook.text: IBM invented the mainframe, built the PC, and dominated enterprise IT for decades. Cloud was supposed to be easy.

buildup.text: IBM launched cloud services early but spread resources across too many initiatives without clear focus.

discovery.text: In 2019, IBM bought Red Hat for $34 billion, betting on hybrid cloud using OpenShift and Linux.

twist.text: IBM Cloud holds under 4% market share. The Red Hat bet is about consulting and hybrid, not competing on scale.

climax.text: IBM's cloud future isn't about being the biggest. It's about being the hybrid and consulting specialist.

punchline.text: IBM can't beat AWS in scale. It's betting on complexity.

quiz.question: Why did IBM acquire Red Hat for $34 billion?
options: To build a hybrid cloud strategy around OpenShift and Linux technologies
To shut down Red Hat's open-source projects
To gain Red Hat's public cloud data centers
